---
title: "When dense retrieval quietly fails at K=10"
description: "On a 4-hop question over 21k chunks, dense retrieval delivers one gold chunk of four; the graph walk pins the bridge chunks it misses. Retrieval is envelope assembly, not question answering."
date: "2026-07-20"
categories: [retrieval]
draft: true
---

::: {.callout-note title="Status"}
**Built and demonstrated.** This runs today; caveats are noted inline.
:::

**Thesis.** On a 4-hop question over 21k chunks, dense retrieval delivers one gold chunk of four; the graph walk pins the bridge chunks it misses. Retrieval is envelope assembly, not question answering.

**Draws on.** Drawn from the wiki's Retrieval-Context-Envelope-Site. Live site and simulation; the full at-scale comparison is still deferred on compute.

## Outline

- 
- 
- 

_Stub. Expand from the seed page(s) above._
